title: Pullback Of The Area Form Represents The Local Degree
summary: The local charge of an isolated node is the degree of the normalized map from a small sphere in momentum space to the target sphere, computed by pulling back the normalized area form.
assumptions: The enclosing sphere does not intersect any other bad point.
The normalized map is well-defined on the sphere.
regime: Degree-theoretic description of an isolated point node.
scope: Local proof fragment that identifies the charge with a pullback integral.

source_anchors.notes: Witten's charge formula is the degree of the normalized map to the target two-sphere.

representation: Local degree formula for the charge of an isolated node.

math_expressions.notes: Charge as the degree of the normalized map \widehat b.
Closedness of the normalized area form on the target sphere.

step_justification: Normalizing the Pauli-vector coefficients defines a map from the enclosing sphere to S^2.
The pullback of the normalized area form measures how many times the domain sphere wraps the target sphere.
This integer is the local charge of the bad point.
retrieval_hints: Use when a proof must move from the local Weyl cone to the degree-theoretic notion of charge.
